---
title: "LRP103: Mechanism model — taught receptive vocabulary (TR) -> letter sounds (L), lagged / predictive"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP103 is the **lagged / predictive** sibling of the concurrent **LRP102**. It takes
taught receptive vocabulary at **period-start** ($TR_{\text{pre}}$) predicting
letter-sound knowledge at **period-end** ($L_{\text{post}}$; DAG symbol $LS$),
conditioning on the child's period-start letter-sound level ($L_{\text{pre}}$), across
all three phase transitions with phase-specific intercepts. Because the predictor
precedes the outcome increment and the outcome's own baseline is held, the slope reads
as "does where a child's taught vocabulary started predict how much their letter sounds
then changed" — the design that actually tests *prediction of letter-sound growth* and
the one behind the descriptive null (baseline $TR \approx -0.1$ with the subsequent $L$
gain). This is the growth-prediction half of #405.

::: {.callout-important title="Exploratory edge-check — not a hypothesised causal path"}
The revised DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`) posits **no vocabulary $\to$
letter-sound edge**, so any association is latent-general-ability-confounded, an
edge-check on the committed DAG, **never** a mechanism. See LRP102 for the full
no-edge argument.
:::

_Lagged form (estimand note)._ The single build-level difference from LRP102 is
`mechanism_at_pre=True`: the mechanism regressor is $TR$'s period-start (pre) logit
rather than its post logit. The outcome stays at post and the autoregressive baseline
stays at $L_{\text{pre}}$, giving
$TR_{\text{pre}} \to L_{\text{post}} \mid L_{\text{pre}}$. A positive concurrent
slope (LRP102) alongside a null lagged slope
here is the expected "tracks, but doesn't drive" signature. The mechanism is a
**linear** slope (vocabulary-exposure precedent LRP56/57, `mech-088`).

## Adjustment set

Identical to the concurrent LRP102 — re-derived for the $TR$--$LS$ backdoor under the
revised DAG: $\{G, A, HS, IS(\text{attend}), SP(\text{deapp\_c})\}$ plus the
$L_{\text{pre}}$ autoregressive baseline, with $GA$ proxied by the child random
intercept. Phonological memory $RW$ (`erbto`) is **not** adjusted (it does not reach
$LS$); $SP$ is kept as a precision adjuster. See [LRP102](../lrp-rli-mech-102/) for the
per-symbol derivation.

### Interpretation caveats

- *No unmeasured confounder assumption* for $(TR_{\text{pre}}, L_{\text{post}})$:
  strong and not testable; latent $GA$ is the named violation direction that cannot be closed.
- The $t2 \to$ later increment is not itself randomised, so this is a triangulation
  read, not a headline causal contrast.
- $TR_{\text{pre}}$ is measured with error; attenuation applies.
- Across-phase pooling assumes a stable structural relationship over time.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
